STEP 4 — Button Component
Decision (final): Button supports 3 variants (primary, secondary, ghost) and 3 sizes (sm, md, lg). Primary uses accent background with white text; secondary is ink outline on paper; ghost is text-only with underline-on-hover. Radius: sm (4px) only — consistent with the sharp/minimal personality. All transitions use transform/opacity/background-color at 150ms per token rules — no width/height animation.

current_payload:
Step 8 (Cart Context) is complete!

1. Created `src/context/CartContext.tsx` with the `"use client"` directive.
2. Defined the exact `CartItem` type as requested (with `size`, `color`, and composite `id`).
3. Set up the `useReducer` state management mapping to `ADD_ITEM` (with auto-incrementing logic for composite IDs), `REMOVE_ITEM`, `UPDATE_QUANTITY`, `CLEAR_CART`, and `HYDRATE`, plus the drawer visibility state (`OPEN_CART`, `CLOSE_CART`).
4. Computed `itemCount` and `subtotal` perfectly derived from the cart items on the fly.
5. Wired up the two `useEffect` hooks: one for hydration from `localStorage` on mount, and one for persisting changes back to `localStorage` (safeguarded via a `hasHydrated` state flag to prevent overwriting with an empty array).
6. Wrapped the app in `src/app/layout.tsx` precisely within the `<body>`.
7. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` (zero errors).
8. Temporarily tested local storage persistence manually using a test button in `page.tsx` and removed it.

Ready for the next step: Building the CartDrawer component.
